title: lentibulariaceae
intro: Lentibulariaceae is a family of flowering plants commonly known as bladderworts. This rank is the level above the genus and below the order in the plant taxonomic hierarchy. It typically consists of aquatic plants that thrive in shallow water, characterized by unique structures such as bladder-like traps and modified leaves that resemble tentacles. The Lentibulariaceae family includes approximately 435 species, many of which are carnivorous and obtain essential nutrients by capturing small insects and other organisms.
